heading: Live Migration Workflow
body:
This section outlines the workflow. For detailed steps,
see the [Live migrate (push) a MongoDB cluster into Atlas](lm-push-c2c-procedure).

!["To live migrate your deployment to Atlas, generate a link-token,
provision a migration host, and start live migration."](/_includes/images/live-migration-c2c-stages.png)

The stages in the live migration workflow are:

- **Stage 1: Select the Migration Path in Atlas**. Perform this step in Atlas,
  after you have created your Atlas account, organization, and
  project; deployed your dedicated cluster in this project; and can
  connect to it.

  - For detailed steps, see the
    [Live migrate (push) a MongoDB cluster into Atlas](lm-push-c2c-procedure).

- **Stage 2: Provision Migration Host**.

  - [provision a migration host](cloud-manager/tutorial/provision-migration-host)
    in Cloud Manager. A migration host runs a dedicated MongoDB Agent
    that orchestrates the live migration process from Cloud Manager to Atlas.

    > **Note:** If you are migrating a source MongoDB deployment that hasn't used
    > Cloud Manager before, [add existing MongoDB processes to Cloud Manager](cloud-manager/tutorial/add-existing-mongodb-processes).

  - In the Live Migration: Connect to Atlas section of your
    Cloud Manager organization's Settings page, select
    Connect to Atlas and paste the link-token
    that you created in Atlas. To learn more, see
    [Connect to Atlas for Live Migration](cloud-manager/tutorial/connect-to-atlas-live-migration) in Cloud Manager.

- **Stage 3: Start the Migration**. In Atlas, follow the
  steps in the wizard to start the live migration process.

Before you begin the live migration procedure, add the IP addresses or
CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) blocks of your migration hosts to the project
[IP Access List](access-list). Atlas allows connections to the
destination cluster only from hosts with entries in the project's
access list.
